name: Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva
role: President of Brazil (2003–2010, 2023–present). Former metalworkers' union leader, political prisoner under the dictatorship, and four-time presidential candidate.
quote: Never before in the history of this country has someone cared so much about the poor.

name: Jair Messias Bolsonaro
role: President of Brazil (2019–2022). Former army captain and 27-year congressman. Far-right politician who promoted election fraud claims and fled to Florida after losing to Lula. Barred from office until 2030 by TSE.
quote: I'm not a gravedigger. If someone dies, everyone dies, it's life. You can't cry about it forever.

name: Dilma Rousseff
role: President of Brazil (2011–2016). First female president, former guerrilla tortured by the military dictatorship. Impeached in 2016 on fiscal irregularity charges; currently heads the BRICS New Development Bank.
quote: What they are doing to me is a coup. There is no crime. There is no legal basis for this impeachment.

name: Fernando Henrique Cardoso
role: President of Brazil (1995–2002). Sociologist and co-creator of the Plano Real. Led Brazil's privatization program, democratic consolidation, and passage of the Fiscal Responsibility Law. Two-term president.
quote: I did not become president to be popular. I became president to make Brazil work.

name: Getúlio Dornelles Vargas
role: President and Dictator of Brazil (1930–1945, 1950–1954). The 'Father of the Poor' and architect of Brazilian industrialization. Creator of the CLT labor law, Petrobras, and the Estado Novo authoritarian state. Died by suicide in 1954.
quote: I choose this means to be always with you. When they humiliate you, you will feel my soul suffering at your side.

name: Dom Pedro II
role: Emperor of Brazil (1831–1889). Ruled for 58 years, Brazil's longest-serving head of state. Modernized the empire, supported abolitionism, led Brazil in the Paraguayan War. Peacefully accepted his own deposition in 1889.
quote: If I were not emperor, I would like to have been a teacher. I know of no nobler or more worthy mission.

name: Sérgio Fernando Moro
role: Federal Judge (2014–2018) who led Operation Lava Jato from Curitiba. Convicted Lula in 2017. Became Bolsonaro's Justice Minister (2019–2020). STF found he acted with bias. Now a senator (Paraná, Podemos).
quote: The law must be applied with firmness, but also with intelligence. There can be no sacred cows.

name: Alexandre de Moraes
role: Minister of the STF (Supreme Court) since 2017. President of the TSE Electoral Court (2022–2024). Led certification of 2022 election results and prosecution of January 8 rioters. Investigating Bolsonaro for coup conspiracy.
quote: Democracy has defenders. It will not be silenced by terrorists.

name: Michel Miguel Elias Temer Lulia
role: President of Brazil (2016–2018) after Dilma's impeachment. Former PMDB party president and career politician. Introduced spending cap (Emenda Constitucional 95) freezing public spending for 20 years. His government enacted major pension and labor reforms.
quote: We did not seek this situation. But when it came to us, we faced it with responsibility.

name: Isabel Cristina Leopoldina de Bragança
role: Princess Imperial of Brazil. Served as regent three times for her father Dom Pedro II. Signed the Lei Áurea abolishing slavery on May 13, 1888. Known as 'A Redentora' (The Redeemer). Exiled to Europe after the 1889 republican coup.
quote: If the abolition of slavery cost me the throne, I do not regret it. It was worth it.

name: Joaquim Aurélio Barreto Nabuco de Araújo
role: Abolitionist, diplomat, and intellectual (1849–1910). Led the Brazilian Anti-Slavery Society. Author of 'O Abolicionismo' (1883), the foundational text of Brazilian abolitionism. Brazil's first ambassador to the United States (1905–1910).
quote: Slavery poisoned everything it touched — the soil, the culture, the character of the master and the fate of the slave.

name: Zumbi dos Palmares
role: Last leader of Quilombo dos Palmares (c.1655–1695). Led resistance against Portuguese colonial forces until the quilombo's destruction. Captured and executed November 20, 1695. Now Brazil's primary symbol of Black resistance; his death date is the National Day of Black Consciousness.
quote: Freedom is not begged. Freedom is conquered.

name: Ernesto Beckmann Geisel
role: Military President of Brazil (1974–1979). Initiated the political 'abertura' (opening), ending AI-5 in 1978 and signing the Amnesty Law in 1979. Led Brazil toward a 'slow, gradual and secure' return to democracy. Oversaw both liberalization and continued political repression.
quote: The path to democracy must be slow, gradual, and secure — lest we lose what we are trying to preserve.

name: Joaquim José da Silva Xavier (Tiradentes)
role: Dentist, muleteer, and junior military officer (c.1746–1792). Leader of the Inconfidência Mineira independence conspiracy (1789). The only conspirator executed — hanged and quartered on April 21, 1792. Brazil's national hero and republican martyr.
quote: I alone am responsible. Do not punish the others — they only followed where I led.

name: Marina Silva
role: Environmentalist and politician. Former rubber tapper, senator, and Environment Minister under Lula I (2003–2008). Presidential candidate in 2010 and 2014 (reached 21.3% in first round 2014). Returned as Environment Minister under Lula III (2023–present), credited with reducing Amazon deforestation.
quote: The Amazon belongs not just to Brazil. It belongs to all humanity. We must protect it together.
